title: Wildfire Smoke PM2.5 Exposure And Respiratory Health Impact In Fire-Prone Regions
summary: Build a verified workflow for estimating wildfire smoke PM2.5 exposure and respiratory health impact, linking satellite-derived smoke plumes, ground-monitor PM2.5, and health-utilization data in fire-prone regions.
why_it_matters: Wildfire smoke contributes up to 25 percent of global PM2.5 and is increasing with climate change. Smoke PM2.5 may be more toxic than other PM2.5 sources. NOAA HMS and GEOS-Chem provide smoke-plume and composition data, but linking smoke exposure to verified health outcomes at sub-national scale is underdeveloped.

success_metrics.target: Every smoke-exposure estimate links satellite, monitor, and health data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
